user.title: Connect to the Brilliant panel
user.description: Enter the panel's IP and root password. If the bridge agent is already installed, its settings are adopted automatically — you're done after this step.

data_description.host: The panel's address on your network (e.g. 192.168.1.10).
data_description.root_password: Stored per panel — each controller can have its own root password.

broker.title: MQTT broker
broker.description: The broker the on-panel agent publishes to. Pre-filled from your most recently added panel.

data_description.name: A friendly name, e.g. "Office Bath". A lowercase id is derived for MQTT topics ("Office Bath" → office-bath).
data_description.mesh_priority: 0 = this panel never publishes the BLE mesh. Among positive values the LOWEST wins leader election (1 = primary, 2/3 = standbys).

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure panel
reconfigure.description: Edit this panel's connection, broker, and mesh priority. Changes are pushed to the panel and the agent is restarted. The panel name can't be changed.

error.reserved_panel: "mesh" is the reserved whole-home pseudo-panel and cannot be managed.
error.invalid_name: Enter a name with at least one letter or digit.
error.invalid_value: Control characters are not allowed in this field.
error.cannot_connect: SSH connection failed — check the host and the root password.
error.cannot_read_config: The agent is installed but its configuration could not be read. Try Reconfigure, or remove and re-add the panel.
error.cannot_apply: Connected, but writing the new settings to the panel failed. Check the agent and try Repair.
error.wrong_panel: That host already runs a different Brilliant panel's agent. Check the IP — applying here would overwrite and restart the wrong panel.
error.host_key_changed: The panel's SSH host key changed since it was added. If this is expected (e.g. the panel was reflashed), remove and re-add the panel to re-pin it. Otherwise it may indicate a man-in-the-middle, so do not proceed until you have confirmed the host.

data.auto_repair: Automatically repair the bridge after an outage

data_description.trust_host_key_changes: When on, repair auto-trusts a changed SSH host key so a firmware update that rotates the panel's keys recovers hands-off. This offers the root password to whatever host answers at the panel's address, so only enable on a trusted/isolated network (e.g. a firewalled IoT VLAN). Each automatic re-pin fires a host_key_repinned event you can alert on. Default off: a changed key instead surfaces repair_failed (host_key_changed) and asks you to Reconfigure to re-pin.

repair.name: Repair bridge
repair.description: Restore the unit/env from known-good sources and start the agent.

redeploy.name: Redeploy agent
redeploy.description: Force-push the bundled agent payload and restart the bridge.

uninstall.name: Uninstall agent
uninstall.description: Stop, disable, and remove the agent from the panel (explicit only).

already_in_progress.message: A repair or update is already in progress for this panel.

host_key_changed.message: The panel SSH host key changed. Open Reconfigure to re-pin it, or enable "Trust host-key changes" in options for hands-off repair.

needs_attention.title: Brilliant panel needs attention
needs_attention.description: Panel `{panel}` needs attention: {reason}. This issue clears automatically once the bridge recovers.
